title: chrysanthellum indicum subsp. mexicanum
intro: Chrysanthellum indicum subsp. mexicanum, also known as the Mexican goldenseal, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Chrysanthellum indicum subsp. mexicanum include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and sometimes used in combination with other herbs for various herbal preparations.
